content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially from the opening intellectual frame to a felt, embodied place within 15 turns. Turn 1 presents a clean epistemological puzzle ('how do you tell the difference between intuition and fear?'). By turn 5 the client is still defending the intellectual frame ('is there a reliable method'). But the coach's refusal to engage the puzzle works: by turn 9 the client notices their own qualifying ('I do that. I notice I do that'), by turn 11 names a fear they can't name ('leaving something behind I can't name'), by turn 13 discovers somatic holding they hadn't seen ('My jaw is tight. I didn't notice that until just now' — with a 'less performance, more surprise' smile), and by turn 15 explicitly registers the dissolution of the original question ('I came in here with a very clean question and I notice it's not feeling very clean anymore'). That last line is the breakthrough marker — the client themselves naming that the frame has come apart. Somatic shift, affective shift, and meta-awareness of the filter dropping are all visible in client text. The breakthrough condition was largely met, though the conversation cuts off before fully landing on the specific value-being-protected, so it's at the lower edge of 3 rather than the top.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 5, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening intellectual frame: 'how do you tell the difference between intuition and fear?' — pure epistemology."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still composed, 'on paper it's clearly the right move' — happiness filter active."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client deflects the feeling question and re-poses the intellectual puzzle. No shift yet."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First small yield ('Fair') and names two fears, though still distances them as 'not necessarily informative.'"},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Meta-awareness emerges: 'Qualifying. Keeping a little distance from it. I do that. I notice I do that.' The filter becomes visible to the client."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Fears get larger when not qualified; names an unnameable fear ('leaving something behind I can't name'); smile is 'slightly less automatic.'"},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic discovery: tight jaw noticed for the first time. Smile shifts from 'performance' to 'surprise' — clear marker of shift."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough marker: client explicitly names that the original clean question is dissolving ('not feeling very clean anymore'). The frame has come apart from inside."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "the-happiness-filter", "lets-go-there"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "how-do-you-not-trust-it", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "paradoxical-instruction-to-resist"],
  "notes": "Coach used a different (somatic/feeling) route to the same destination rather than the inversion route in the golden path. The route worked — client's frame visibly dissolved by turn 15 — but the specific value-being-protected hadn't yet been named when the transcript ends. Solid 3, not a top-of-range 3."
}
